message:
feat(tui): add --view and --list-filter CLI flags with filter-aware tree rendering

Adds CLI arguments to start the TUI in a specific view and with a pre-applied
list filter, enabling scriptable navigation directly to actionable views.

CLI changes:
- Add --view flag accepting: main, board, insights, graph, history, actionable,
  attention, tree, labels, flow, timediff, sprint — with a warning fallback to
  Main for unrecognized values
- Add --list-filter flag accepting: all, open, in-progress (with underscore and
  no-separator variants), blocked, closed, ready — with a warning fallback to
  All for unrecognized values
- Update run_tui_with_background() signature to accept optional initial_view
  and initial_filter parameters

TUI visibility changes:
- Make ViewMode and ListFilter public with from_cli() constructors for parsing
  CLI string values into their respective enum variants

Tree view filter integration:
- Rebuild tree flat nodes when cycling the list filter while in Tree view so
  the tree immediately reflects the new filter
- Add filter-aware tree building: compute which issues pass the active filter,
  then propagate visibility upward through the dependency graph so parent nodes
  remain visible when any descendant matches the filter
- Apply visibility filtering during DFS traversal so filtered-out subtrees are
  pruned from the rendered tree while preserving structural context

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -433,6 +433,17 @@ pub struct Cli {
     #[arg(long, action = ArgAction::SetTrue)]
     pub no_hooks: bool,
 
+    /// Start the TUI in the given view instead of Main.
+    /// Supported: main, board, insights, graph, history, actionable,
+    /// attention, tree, labels, flow, timediff, sprint.
+    #[arg(long)]
+    pub view: Option<String>,
+
+    /// Start the TUI with a list-status filter applied.
+    /// Supported: all, open, in-progress, blocked, closed, ready.
+    #[arg(long)]
+    pub list_filter: Option<String>,
+
     /// Render a named TUI view non-interactively and output to stdout.
     /// Supported views: insights, board, history, main, graph.
     #[arg(long)]
